one_liner: FNMA at $6.10 is a pure policy speculation play — no DCF, no grade, RSI 44.4 below EMAs — avoid unless you have high conviction on a specific government privatization timeline.

price_action.intraday_story: FNMA moved +4.1% on near-average volume with no specific news. The stock remains in its Wyckoff Phase A-B range ($6.00-$8.55). RSI at 44.4 is below 50 — not technically strong.

news_catalyst.headline: No FNMA-Specific News — Trading on General Housing Market Sentiment
news_catalyst.summary: No specific Fannie Mae news. FNMA is a political/policy speculation stock whose value depends on the US government's privatization stance. The stock trades erratically on any housing or GSE policy commentary.

technical.summary: Below both EMAs with negative MACD. Policy speculation stock — not suitable for technical analysis frameworks. Avoid.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Phase A-B but below both EMAs. Policy-driven stock — Wyckoff signals less meaningful here.

valuation.summary: No DCF applicable — FNMA is a government-sponsored enterprise in conservatorship. Value is entirely dependent on policy decisions.

key_concerns: Zero fundamental analysis possible — policy speculation only
fundamentals.summary: No meaningful fundamental grade. Pure policy speculation on GSE privatization.

risks: Government policy changes can move this 20-30% in a single day
